message:
beads: file bd-48wuw — reject embedded NUL in path components

File a [HIGH] priority bug in .beads/issues.jsonl tracking a
real path-validation gap surfaced during a recent reviewer pass:

ffs-core's validate_single_path_component (crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs
:14500) currently only rejects empty components, embedded slashes,
and names longer than 255 bytes. Names containing embedded b"\0"
return Ok and propagate into both ext4 and btrfs write paths
(create / mknod / mkdir / unlink / link / symlink / rename), which
breaks POSIX path-component semantics — kernel ext4/btrfs cannot
address NUL-containing names through a normal syscall path because
the kernel terminates path strings at the NUL.

The bead also records a downstream finding: fuzz_path_component_validation
(fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_path_component_validation.rs:129-148) only
models {Empty, ContainsSlash, TooLong, Valid}, so embedded-NUL names
classify as Valid, and commit e0d2a78 actually pinned that buggy
behavior with a corpus seed (seed_embedded_nul) whose oracle says
"valid". Acceptance work documented in the bead:

- Reject components containing b"\0" before inode allocation /
  directory mutation across ext4 + btrfs create / mkdir / mknod /
  link / symlink / unlink / rmdir / rename old & new.
- Update the fuzz oracle so embedded-NUL is classified invalid.
- Add ext4 + btrfs regression tests using
  OsStr::from_bytes(b"...\0...") asserting failure and unchanged
  parent-dir state.

Filing only — no source fix in this commit. The bead is the
work item we'll assign next.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
